title: Human Rights Flaws in Nigerian Resource Extraction: A Synoptic Review of Scholarly Works
author: Kennedy-Kirk, Stanley
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Nigeria's resource extraction projects have significantly impacted human rights across various sectors. A critical analysis of existing literature focusing on legal frameworks, case studies, and policy recommendations related to resource extraction in Nigeria. The reviewed studies highlight widespread violations such as forced displacement and environmental degradation, with significant proportions affecting marginalized communities. Current research underscores the urgent need for comprehensive human rights protections and equitable distribution of benefits from resource extraction projects. Policy recommendations include strengthening legal frameworks, enhancing accountability mechanisms, and promoting community participation in decision-making processes.
